title: gcp-sm: validate_url_path_component only blocks # and %, not full GCP charset
description: GCP project IDs allow [a-z0-9-], secret names allow [A-Za-z0-9_-]. The validator only rejects # and %. A URI with a space (decoded from %20) passes validation, gets interpolated as a space into the REST URL path, and produces a confusing HTTP 400 from GCP instead of InvalidUri at construction time. The validator correctly blocks the dangerous injection cases (# truncates the URL path) but gives false confidence that any non-rejected component is URL-safe. GCP enforces character constraints server-side so this never silently returns wrong data, but the error message quality is poor.
